evals.prompt: I have a folder called ./application_materials with my CV, a rough SOP, project notes, and a target-school list. Read it and build a reusable academic profile I can use for PhD SOPs and professor emails.
evals.expected_output: A source-grounded applicant profile with source inventory, applicant snapshot, research identity, evidence inventory, narrative options, downstream use notes, and prioritized missing information.

evals.prompt: Can you read my academic background from these files before we work on my CV? I have a resume, transcript notes, GitHub project summaries, and a list of recommenders. I want to know what evidence is strongest for research applications.
evals.expected_output: A structured academic-career evidence inventory that separates facts from interpretation, identifies strongest research-readiness signals, and flags missing details before CV work.

evals.prompt: I'm applying to research master's and maybe PhD programs later. Please read my profile notes and tell me what story my materials currently support, what programs/faculty direction would fit, and what information is missing.
evals.expected_output: A profile synthesis with research narrative options, target-program fit clues, constraints, downstream use guidance, and high-priority follow-up questions.
